title: HTML Forms and PHP
description: How HTML forms send data to PHP scripts.

slides.title: Forms: User Input
slides.content:
# HTML Forms

Forms allow users to enter data and send it to the server. A basic form looks like this:

```html
<form action="process.php" method="post">
    <label for="name">Name:</label>
    <input type="text" name="username" id="name">
    <input type="submit" value="Submit">
</form>
```

*   `action`: the URL where the form data is sent.
*   `method`: HTTP method (GET or POST).
*   `name` attribute on inputs: the key used in PHP to access the value.

slides.title: Simple Form Example
slides.content:
Create a file `form.html`: ```html
Name:
Email:
``` Create `welcome.php`: ```php Hello !
Your email is . ``` When the form is submitted, the data is available in `$_POST`.
slides.codeSnippet:
Name:
Email:
Hello !
Your email is .

quizData.question: In an HTML form, what attribute specifies where to send the data?
quizData.explanation: The action attribute contains the URL of the script that will process the form.
